title: Seeing a Football Match in Berlin: Quick Summary
items: **It is the opposite of what most visitors assume. Union Berlin are the Bundesliga club, Hertha BSC are in the 2. Bundesliga.** Union play at Stadion An der Alten Försterei in Köpenick, which holds 22,012. Hertha play at the Olympiastadion, built for around 74,000.

**Hertha is the game you can actually get into.** For the 15 August 2026 home opener against Heidenheim, tickets are listed from 30 to 75 EUR and general sale starts on 24 July. Prices and offers vary by fixture, but availability is usually far easier than at Union.

**Union tickets are the hardest in the city, and the rules are strict.** Members buy first, the tickets are personalised and cannot be passed on, and anything bought on a resale marketplace is void at the turnstile. Standing starts around 15 EUR when you can get it.

**2026/27 is the last season at the Alte Försterei as it stands.** 18,395 of its 22,012 places are standing, on terraces the fans rebuilt themselves in 2008 and 2009. Union move to the Olympiastadion for 2027/28 while the ground is enlarged.

**The overlooked option is Union Berlin Frauen**, top-flight football at the same Köpenick ground with none of the ticket scramble. Their season opens at home against Bayern Munich on Friday 21 August 2026 at 18:20.

**You cannot pin a kickoff time down months ahead.** The leagues published the 2026/27 fixture lists on 2 July 2026, but they fix only the weekend. Exact times land a few weeks out, so a game can move between Friday night and Sunday evening.

**Getting there:** S3 from Alexanderplatz to S Köpenick then a 15 minute walk for Union, U2 to Olympia-Stadion then 5 minutes for Hertha. Both are about 40 minutes from the centre and inside the normal AB fare area.

**At the gate:** nothing bigger than A4, no backpacks at Union, gates open 120 minutes before kickoff at Union and 90 at Hertha. Both take cash and normal cards, and neither uses a stadium payment card.
